examples.text: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَى قَوْمِهِ فَلَبِثَ فِيهِمْ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ عَامًا فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلطُّوفَانُ وَهُمْ ظَالِمُونَ فَأَنْجَيْنَاهُ وَأَصْحَابَ ٱلسَّفِينَةِ وَجَعَلْنَاهَا آيَةً لِلْعَالَمِينَ
examples.english: And We certainly sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them nine hundred and fifty years, and the flood seized them while they were wrongdoers. But We saved him and the companions of the ship, and We made it a sign for the worlds.

examples.text:
10th century, Al-Mutanabbi
مَا كُلُّ مَا يَتَمَنَّى ٱلْمَرْءُ يُدْرِكُهُ
تَجْرِي ٱلرِّيَاحُ بِمَا لَا تَشْتَهِي ٱلسُّفُنُ
mā kullu mā yatamannā l-marʔu yudrikuhu
tajrī r-riyāḥu bimā lā taštahī s-sufunu
examples.english: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

etymology.text: From Aramaic סְפינִתָּא / ܣܦܻܝܢܬܳܐ (sǝp̄īntā). Forming the root س ف ن (s f n). Cognate with Hebrew סְפִינָה (sᵊfīnāh).

body.text: an adz with which palm-trunks are pared;
such as the skins of crocodiles,
which is put upon the hilts of swords:
which is a rough skin, wherewith whips and arrows are rubbed
and which is upon the hilts of swords:
a rough piece of the skin of the
of the skin of a fish, with which the arrow is rubbed so as to remove from it the marks of the paring-knife:
(M, L, K:) or, as some say, (M, L,)
a stone with which one shapes out,
(M, L, K:) sometimes, accord. to Lth,
an iron implement with which one rubs wood so as to smooth it:
a cane which is hollowed, and has some notches cut in it, through which an arrow is put and repeatedly drawn
.) See an ex. in a verse cited in art.
